tasks.title: Setup Project Infrastructure and Environment
tasks.description: Initialize the project repository with the required tech stack and development environment for the AutoProof platform.
tasks.details:
Create a new repository with the following structure and configuration:

1. Frontend: React 18.2.0 with TypeScript 5.0+, Tailwind CSS 3.3.0
   - Use Vite 4.4+ as the build tool for faster development
   - Configure ESLint and Prettier for code quality
   - Setup React Router v6.14+ for routing

2. Backend: FastAPI 0.103.0 with Python 3.11
   - Create virtual environment with poetry or pipenv
   - Configure SQLAlchemy 2.0+ for ORM
   - Setup pydantic 2.3+ for data validation
   - Add CORS middleware for frontend communication

3. Database: Supabase PostgreSQL
   - Create Supabase project
   - Configure connection pooling
   - Setup initial migration scripts

4. Authentication: Clerk integration
   - Register application with Clerk
   - Configure JWT middleware
   - Setup authentication hooks in frontend

5. CI/CD: GitHub Actions
   - Configure linting, testing, and build workflows
   - Setup deployment pipeline to chosen hosting platform

6. Environment Variables:
   - Create .env.example file with required variables
   - Configure environment variable loading in both frontend and backend

Implementation should follow a monorepo structure with separate directories for frontend and backend.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify all dependencies install correctly with `npm install` (frontend) and `pip install` (backend)
2. Ensure development servers start without errors
3. Validate connection to Supabase database
4. Test Clerk authentication flow
5. Verify CI/CD pipeline runs successfully on test commit
6. Confirm environment variables are properly loaded in both environments

subtasks.title: Frontend Scaffolding and Configuration
subtasks.description: Initialize the frontend application using React, Vite, and TypeScript. Set up Tailwind CSS for styling, configure ESLint and Prettier for code quality, and integrate React Router for routing.
subtasks.details: Create the frontend directory within the monorepo. Scaffold the React app with Vite, install and configure Tailwind CSS, ESLint, Prettier, and React Router. Ensure TypeScript is enabled and all configurations are committed.

subtasks.title: Backend Scaffolding and Configuration
subtasks.description: Set up the backend using FastAPI and Python 3.11. Create a virtual environment, install SQLAlchemy for ORM, Pydantic for data validation, and configure CORS middleware.
subtasks.details: Create the backend directory within the monorepo. Initialize a Python virtual environment using poetry or pipenv. Install FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, Pydantic, and CORS dependencies. Set up initial FastAPI app structure.

subtasks.title: Database Provisioning and Migration Setup
subtasks.description: Provision a Supabase PostgreSQL database, configure connection pooling, and set up migration tooling for schema management.
subtasks.details: Create a Supabase project, obtain connection credentials, and configure pooling. Integrate migration tools (e.g., Alembic for Python) in the backend. Document migration workflow.

subtasks.title: Authentication Provider Integration
subtasks.description: Integrate Clerk authentication for both frontend and backend, enabling user management and JWT validation.
subtasks.details: Install Clerk SDKs in both frontend and backend. Set up ClerkProvider in React, implement authentication flows, and add JWT validation middleware in FastAPI.

subtasks.title: CI/CD Pipeline Configuration
subtasks.description: Configure GitHub Actions workflows to automate linting, testing, building, and deploying both frontend and backend applications.
subtasks.details: Create workflow YAML files in the monorepo root. Set up jobs for linting, running tests, building artifacts, and deploying to target environments. Ensure both frontend and backend pipelines are covered.

subtasks.title: Environment Variable Management
subtasks.description: Establish a secure and consistent approach for managing environment variables across frontend and backend, including .env.example files and loading mechanisms.
subtasks.details: Create .env.example files in both frontend and backend directories. Implement environment variable loading using appropriate libraries (e.g., dotenv). Document required variables and usage.

subtasks.title: Monorepo Structure and Tooling
subtasks.description: Establish the monorepo structure using a tool like pnpm or Turborepo, configure workspaces, and set up shared tooling and scripts.
subtasks.details: Create the root monorepo directory, add workspace configuration (e.g., pnpm-workspace.yaml), and set up shared scripts, linting, and formatting rules at the root level.

subtasks.title: Initial Documentation and Developer Onboarding
subtasks.description: Write initial documentation covering project structure, setup instructions, development workflows, and onboarding steps for new contributors.
subtasks.details: Create a README.md at the root of the monorepo. Document setup steps for frontend, backend, database, authentication, CI/CD, environment variables, and monorepo tooling. Include troubleshooting and contribution guidelines.

tasks.title: Implement Core Database Models
tasks.description: Create and migrate the database schema for core entities including PolicyRule and ViolationLog tables as specified in the PRD.
tasks.details:
Using SQLAlchemy and Supabase PostgreSQL, implement the following database models:

1. Teams Table:
```sql
CREATE TABLE teams (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  subscription_tier VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'starter',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

2. PolicyRule Table (as specified in PRD):
```sql
CREATE TABLE policy_rules (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  team_id UUID REFERENCES teams(id),
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  keywords JSONB,
  conditions JSONB,
  severity VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'medium',
  active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

3. ViolationLog Table (as specified in PRD):
```sql
CREATE TABLE violation_logs (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  team_id UUID REFERENCES teams(id),
  rule_id UUID REFERENCES policy_rules(id),
  source VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  content_hash VARCHAR(64),
  severity VARCHAR(20),
  description TEXT,
  recommendation TEXT,
  resolved BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

4. Additional required tables:
```sql
CREATE TABLE api_keys (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  team_id UUID REFERENCES teams(id),
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  key_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  last_used TIMESTAMP,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  revoked BOOLEAN DEFAULT false
);

CREATE TABLE usage_logs (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  team_id UUID REFERENCES teams(id),
  api_key_id UUID REFERENCES api_keys(id) NULL,
  endpoint VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  tokens_used INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

Implement corresponding SQLAlchemy models in Python with proper relationships and type hints. Create Alembic migration scripts for version control of the database schema.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Create unit tests for each model to verify field constraints and relationships
2. Test migration scripts for both up and down migrations
3. Verify foreign key constraints work correctly
4. Test JSONB field serialization and deserialization
5. Validate UUID generation
6. Ensure timestamp fields are automatically populated
7. Test database indexes for query performance

tasks.title: Implement Authentication with Clerk
tasks.description: Integrate Clerk authentication service for user management, JWT validation, and session handling across the application.
tasks.details:
Implement authentication using Clerk with the following components:

1. Frontend Integration:
   - Install @clerk/clerk-react v4.23+ package
   - Create ClerkProvider wrapper in main App component
   - Implement SignIn, SignUp, and UserProfile components
   - Add protected route middleware using useAuth hook
   - Create user onboarding flow after successful authentication

2. Backend Integration:
   - Install clerk-sdk-python package
   - Implement JWT validation middleware for FastAPI
   - Create authentication dependency for protected routes
   - Setup webhook handler for Clerk events (user created, updated, deleted)

3. Team Association:
   - Create logic to associate users with teams
   - Implement team invitation and management
   - Store Clerk user_id in database for reference

4. Session Management:
   - Configure session duration and refresh policies
   - Implement client-side session persistence
   - Handle session expiration gracefully

5. Authorization:
   - Define role-based permissions (admin, member)
   - Implement permission checks in API endpoints
   - Create UI components that adapt to user permissions

Ensure proper error handling for authentication failures and implement secure logout functionality.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test user registration and login flows
2. Verify JWT validation works correctly for protected routes
3. Test session persistence and expiration
4. Validate team association and invitation process
5. Verify role-based permissions function correctly
6. Test authentication error handling
7. Ensure logout properly clears session data
8. Validate Clerk webhook handlers process events correctly

subtasks.title: Integrate Clerk on the Frontend and Implement UI Components
subtasks.description: Install Clerk's React SDK, wrap the main App with ClerkProvider, and implement SignIn, SignUp, and UserProfile components. Add protected route logic using Clerk's hooks and components.
subtasks.details: Use @clerk/clerk-react. Implement <SignIn />, <SignUp />, and <UserProfile /> components. Protect routes with <SignedIn />, <SignedOut />, and <RedirectToSignIn />. Set up ClerkProvider at the root of the app.

subtasks.title: Set Up Backend Clerk SDK and JWT Middleware
subtasks.description: Install Clerk SDK for the backend (e.g., clerk-sdk-python), and implement JWT validation middleware to secure API endpoints.
subtasks.details: Install clerk-sdk-python. Create middleware for JWT validation in FastAPI. Ensure protected routes require valid Clerk-issued JWTs.

subtasks.title: Implement Webhook Event Handling
subtasks.description: Set up webhook endpoints to handle Clerk events such as user creation, deletion, and updates. Process these events to synchronize user data.
subtasks.details: Create webhook endpoints in the backend. Validate Clerk webhook signatures. Handle events like user.created, user.deleted, and update internal records accordingly.

subtasks.title: Develop User-to-Team Association Logic
subtasks.description: Implement logic to associate authenticated users with teams, including database schema and API endpoints for managing associations.
subtasks.details: Design user-team relationship in the database. Create endpoints to add/remove users from teams. Ensure associations are updated on relevant Clerk events.

subtasks.title: Build Team Invitation and Management Features
subtasks.description: Implement team invitation flows, allowing users to invite others, accept invitations, and manage team membership.
subtasks.details: Create invitation endpoints and UI. Send invitation emails. Handle invitation acceptance and update team membership. Provide team management UI for owners/admins.

subtasks.title: Implement Session Management and Persistence
subtasks.description: Ensure session persistence across frontend and backend, handling session expiration, renewal, and revocation.
subtasks.details: Leverage Clerk's session management features. Ensure sessions persist across reloads and devices. Implement session revocation and renewal logic.

subtasks.title: Implement Role-Based Authorization (Backend and Frontend)
subtasks.description: Define roles and permissions, enforce them in both backend APIs and frontend UI components.
subtasks.details: Configure roles in Clerk dashboard. Use Clerk's Protect component and permission checks on frontend. Enforce role checks in backend endpoints for sensitive actions.

subtasks.title: Add Error Handling and Secure Logout Functionality
subtasks.description: Implement robust error handling for authentication flows and provide secure logout mechanisms.
subtasks.details: Handle authentication errors gracefully in UI and API responses. Implement secure logout using Clerk's signOut method and ensure session/token invalidation.

tasks.title: Implement Policy Document Parsing Service
tasks.description: Create a service that parses policy documents (PDF, text, JSON) and extracts structured compliance rules using GPT-4.
tasks.details:
Implement a policy document parsing service with the following components:

1. Document Upload Handling:
   - Create API endpoint POST /api/parse-policy
   - Support multipart/form-data for file uploads
   - Validate file types (PDF, TXT, JSON)
   - Implement file size limits (10MB max)

2. Text Extraction:
   - For PDF: Use PyPDF2 3.0+ or pdfminer.six 20221105+ for text extraction
   - For TXT: Direct text reading
   - For JSON: Parse and validate against expected schema

3. GPT-4 Integration:
   - Use OpenAI Python SDK 1.0+
   - Create prompt engineering for policy rule extraction
   - Example prompt structure:
   ```
   You are a compliance rule extractor. Extract structured rules from the following policy document:
   
   {document_text}
   
   For each rule, provide:
   1. A short identifier/name
   2. A clear description
   3. Related keywords
   4. Specific conditions that would violate this rule
   5. Severity level (low, medium, high, critical)
   
   Format your response as a JSON array of rule objects.
   ```

4. Response Processing:
   - Parse GPT-4 response into structured rule objects
   - Validate extracted rules against expected schema
   - Store rules in PolicyRule table with team_id association

5. Error Handling:
   - Implement retry logic for OpenAI API calls
   - Handle malformed documents gracefully
   - Provide meaningful error messages for failed parsing

6. Optimization:
   - Implement chunking for large documents
   - Add caching for repeated parsing attempts
   - Use background task processing for large documents

Ensure the service meets the 90% accuracy requirement for standard policy templates.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test file upload with various document types and sizes
2. Verify text extraction accuracy from different PDF formats
3. Test GPT-4 prompt with sample policy documents
4. Validate rule extraction accuracy against known policy documents
5. Measure parsing time and optimize for performance
6. Test error handling with malformed or corrupt documents
7. Verify database storage of extracted rules
8. Test concurrent parsing requests for stability

subtasks.title: Implement Document Upload API and Validation
subtasks.description: Develop a POST /api/parse-policy endpoint supporting multipart/form-data for file uploads. Validate file types (PDF, TXT, JSON) and enforce a 10MB file size limit.
subtasks.details: Ensure robust input validation and clear error messages for unsupported file types or oversized files.

subtasks.title: Develop PDF Text Extraction Logic
subtasks.description: Integrate PDF text extraction using PyPDF2 3.0+ or pdfminer.six 20221105+ to handle various PDF formats and encodings.
subtasks.details: Handle edge cases such as corrupt or password-protected PDFs, and ensure extracted text is accurate and complete.

subtasks.title: Implement TXT and JSON Parsing
subtasks.description: For TXT files, read text directly. For JSON files, parse and validate against the expected schema.
subtasks.details: Ensure schema validation for JSON and proper error handling for malformed files.

subtasks.title: Design GPT-4 Prompt Engineering Strategy
subtasks.description: Craft effective prompts and system instructions for GPT-4 to extract structured compliance rules from input text.
subtasks.details: Iterate on prompt design to maximize extraction accuracy and clarity, following best practices for system instructions and prompt engineering[2].

subtasks.title: Integrate GPT-4 API
subtasks.description: Connect to the GPT-4 API, sending crafted prompts and handling authentication, rate limits, and response formats.
subtasks.details: Ensure secure API key management and robust handling of API errors and timeouts[1][3].

subtasks.title: Parse and Validate GPT-4 Responses
subtasks.description: Extract structured compliance rules from GPT-4 responses, validating against the expected schema and handling inconsistencies.
subtasks.details: Implement logic to detect and handle malformed or incomplete responses.

subtasks.title: Store Extracted Rules in Database
subtasks.description: Persist validated compliance rules in the database, ensuring schema consistency and traceability to source documents.
subtasks.details: Design database schema for rules and implement storage logic.

subtasks.title: Implement Error Handling and Retry Mechanisms
subtasks.description: Add comprehensive error handling for all stages, including file parsing, GPT-4 API calls, and database operations. Implement retry logic for transient failures.
subtasks.details: Log errors with sufficient context for debugging and provide user-friendly error messages.

subtasks.title: Optimize Performance (Chunking, Caching, Background Tasks)
subtasks.description: Implement chunking for large documents, caching for repeated requests, and background processing for long-running tasks to improve throughput and responsiveness.
subtasks.details: Ensure the system can handle large files and high concurrency efficiently.

tasks.title: Implement Rule Configuration Interface
tasks.description: Create an admin interface for reviewing and editing parsed policy rules with activation/deactivation functionality.
tasks.details:
Develop a rule configuration interface with the following features:

1. Rule Listing Page:
   - Create React component for /dashboard/policies route
   - Implement data fetching with React Query 5.0+
   - Display rules in a sortable, filterable table
   - Show key information: name, description, severity, status
   - Add pagination for large rule sets (20 per page)

2. Rule Editing:
   - Create modal or dedicated page for rule editing
   - Implement form with validation using React Hook Form 7.45+
   - Fields: name, description, keywords (tag input), conditions (JSON editor), severity (dropdown)
   - Add syntax highlighting for JSON fields using react-json-view or similar

3. Rule Activation Controls:
   - Add toggle switch for rule activation/deactivation
   - Implement batch operations for multiple rules
   - Show visual indicators for active/inactive rules

4. Custom Rule Creation:
   - Add "Create Rule" button and form
   - Implement guided creation process
   - Provide templates for common rule types

5. Rule Validation:
   - Client-side validation for required fields
   - Server-side validation before saving
   - Show validation errors inline

6. Backend API:
   - Create CRUD endpoints for policy rules
   - Implement proper authorization checks
   - Add validation middleware

Ensure the interface is responsive and follows the design principles outlined in the PRD.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test rule listing with various filter and sort combinations
2. Verify rule editing functionality saves correctly
3. Test activation/deactivation toggle
4. Validate custom rule creation process
5. Test form validation for various input scenarios
6. Verify batch operations work correctly
7. Test API endpoints with valid and invalid data
8. Ensure proper error handling and user feedback

tasks.title: Implement Slack Integration
tasks.description: Develop the Slack integration for monitoring messages in real-time and sending alerts for policy violations.
tasks.details:
Implement the Slack integration with the following components:

1. Slack App Configuration:
   - Create Slack App in Slack API dashboard
   - Configure OAuth scopes: channels:read, chat:write, im:write
   - Setup Event Subscriptions for message.channels, message.groups
   - Generate and store client credentials securely

2. OAuth Flow:
   - Create frontend integration page for Slack connection
   - Implement OAuth redirect handler
   - Store access tokens securely in database
   - Handle token refresh and revocation

3. Webhook Endpoint:
   - Create POST /api/webhooks/slack endpoint
   - Implement signature verification using SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET
   - Process incoming events asynchronously
   - Return 200 OK response immediately to acknowledge receipt

4. Message Processing:
   - Extract message content and metadata
   - Filter out bot messages and irrelevant events
   - Send message text to analysis service
   - Process analysis results

5. Alert Mechanism:
   - Implement private DM alerts using chat.postMessage API
   - Format violation messages with actionable information
   - Include links to dashboard for more details
   - Add reaction to original message (optional)

6. Channel Configuration:
   - Allow teams to specify which channels to monitor
   - Implement channel-specific rule sets
   - Store configuration in database

7. Rate Limiting and Quotas:
   - Implement token bucket rate limiting
   - Track usage against subscription limits
   - Handle overages according to pricing policy

Ensure the integration meets the 5-second alert delivery requirement specified in the PRD.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test webhook signature verification with valid and invalid signatures
2. Verify OAuth flow completes successfully
3. Test message event processing with various message types
4. Measure end-to-end processing time from message to alert
5. Verify DM alerts are formatted correctly
6. Test channel configuration changes take effect
7. Validate rate limiting behavior
8. Test error handling for API failures

subtasks.title: Slack App Creation and OAuth Scopes
subtasks.description: Create a new Slack app in the Slack API dashboard, configure required OAuth scopes (channels:read, chat:write, im:write), set up event subscriptions, and securely generate/store client credentials.
subtasks.details: Ensure the app is registered with the correct permissions and event subscriptions for message monitoring and alerting. Store credentials in a secure secrets manager.

subtasks.title: OAuth Flow and Token Storage
subtasks.description: Implement the OAuth 2.0 flow for Slack, including frontend integration, redirect handling, and secure storage of access tokens.
subtasks.details: Build the frontend connection page, handle OAuth redirects, exchange codes for tokens, and store tokens securely in the database with proper encryption.

subtasks.title: Webhook Endpoint and Signature Verification
subtasks.description: Develop a webhook endpoint to receive Slack events and implement signature verification to ensure authenticity.
subtasks.details: Set up a public endpoint for Slack event delivery, verify requests using Slack's signing secret, and reject invalid or replayed requests.

subtasks.title: Event Processing and Filtering
subtasks.description: Process incoming Slack events, filter relevant message events, and discard or ignore irrelevant or duplicate events.
subtasks.details: Implement logic to handle only message events from monitored channels or groups, deduplicate events, and validate event payloads.

subtasks.title: Message Analysis Integration
subtasks.description: Integrate the message analysis pipeline to evaluate Slack messages for policy violations.
subtasks.details: Pass filtered message content to the analysis engine, handle asynchronous processing, and manage analysis results for alerting.

subtasks.title: Alert DM Formatting and Delivery
subtasks.description: Format alert messages using Slack's Block Kit or mrkdwn and deliver direct messages (DMs) to relevant users.
subtasks.details: Design alert templates for clarity, use Slack's formatting best practices, and send DMs via chat:write and im:write scopes.

subtasks.title: Channel Configuration UI and Backend
subtasks.description: Build a UI and backend for configuring which Slack channels are monitored and how alerts are routed.
subtasks.details: Provide a user interface for selecting channels, saving preferences, and updating backend configuration for channel monitoring.

subtasks.title: Rate Limiting and Quota Enforcement
subtasks.description: Implement rate limiting and quota enforcement for Slack API usage and alert delivery to prevent abuse and comply with Slack limits.
subtasks.details: Track API calls and alert frequency per workspace/user, enforce limits, and provide feedback or backoff when limits are reached.

subtasks.title: Error Handling and Monitoring
subtasks.description: Add comprehensive error handling and monitoring for all Slack integration components.
subtasks.details: Log errors, handle Slack API failures gracefully, set up alerting for integration issues, and monitor end-to-end processing health.

tasks.title: Implement GitHub Integration
tasks.description: Develop the GitHub integration for analyzing commits and pull requests, with the ability to block merges for severe violations.
tasks.details:
Implement the GitHub integration with the following components:

1. GitHub App Configuration:
   - Create GitHub App in GitHub Developer settings
   - Configure permissions: contents:read, pull_requests:write, checks:write
   - Setup webhook events: push, pull_request
   - Generate and store client credentials securely

2. Installation Flow:
   - Create frontend integration page for GitHub connection
   - Handle app installation callback
   - Store installation tokens securely in database
   - Associate installations with teams

3. Webhook Endpoint:
   - Create POST /api/webhooks/github endpoint
   - Implement signature verification using GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET
   - Process incoming events asynchronously
   - Return 200 OK response immediately to acknowledge receipt

4. Commit Analysis:
   - For push events, retrieve commit diffs using GitHub API
   - Extract code changes and commit messages
   - Send content to analysis service
   - Store results in violation_logs table

5. Pull Request Analysis:
   - For pull_request events, analyze PR description and title
   - Retrieve changed files and analyze diffs
   - Create check runs using GitHub Checks API
   - Block merges for severe violations by failing check

6. Repository Configuration:
   - Allow teams to specify which repositories to monitor
   - Implement repository-specific rule sets
   - Store configuration in database

7. Violation Reporting:
   - Generate detailed reports with file/line references
   - Add PR comments for specific violations
   - Provide actionable recommendations

Implement proper error handling and ensure the analysis completes before PR merge as specified in the PRD.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test webhook signature verification with valid and invalid signatures
2. Verify app installation flow completes successfully
3. Test commit event processing with various commit types
4. Validate PR analysis and check run creation
5. Test merge blocking for severe violations
6. Verify repository configuration changes take effect
7. Validate violation reporting with file/line references
8. Test error handling for API failures

subtasks.title: GitHub App Creation and Permissions
subtasks.description: Register the GitHub App in the GitHub Developer settings, configure required permissions (contents:read, pull_requests:write, checks:write), set up webhook events (push, pull_request), and securely generate/store client credentials.
subtasks.details: Ensure the app is created with the minimum necessary permissions and that webhook events are configured to trigger on relevant repository activities. Store client secrets securely using environment variables or a secrets manager.

subtasks.title: Installation Flow and Token Management
subtasks.description: Implement the frontend integration page for GitHub connection, handle the app installation callback, and securely store installation tokens in the database.
subtasks.details: Guide users through the GitHub App installation process, handle OAuth or JWT-based authentication as required, and ensure tokens are stored securely for future API requests.

subtasks.title: Webhook Endpoint and Signature Verification
subtasks.description: Develop a secure webhook endpoint to receive GitHub events and implement signature verification to ensure payload authenticity.
subtasks.details: Use HTTPS for the webhook endpoint, verify the X-Hub-Signature-256 header using the app's webhook secret, and reject requests with invalid signatures to prevent spoofing[1].

subtasks.title: Commit Analysis Logic
subtasks.description: Implement logic to analyze commits received via webhook events for policy violations or compliance issues.
subtasks.details: Parse commit data from push events, extract relevant information (author, message, diff), and run analysis routines to detect violations.

subtasks.title: Pull Request Analysis and Check Runs
subtasks.description: Analyze pull requests for violations and create GitHub check runs to report analysis results.
subtasks.details: On pull_request events, analyze the PR's commits and files, then use the Checks API to create or update check runs with detailed results and annotations.

subtasks.title: Repository Configuration UI and Backend
subtasks.description: Build a UI and backend for repository-specific configuration of analysis rules and integration settings.
subtasks.details: Allow users to customize which rules are enforced per repository, manage integration settings, and persist configurations in the backend.

subtasks.title: Violation Reporting and PR Comments
subtasks.description: Implement logic to report violations by posting comments on pull requests and commits as appropriate.
subtasks.details: Use the GitHub API to post informative comments on PRs or commits when violations are detected, including actionable details for remediation.

subtasks.title: Merge Blocking Logic
subtasks.description: Enforce merge blocking for severe violations by failing check runs or using required status checks.
subtasks.details: Configure check runs to fail when severe violations are found, ensuring that GitHub's branch protection rules prevent merging until issues are resolved.

subtasks.title: Error Handling and Monitoring
subtasks.description: Implement robust error handling, logging, and monitoring for all integration components.
subtasks.details: Log errors and webhook failures, monitor API rate limits[3], and set up alerting for integration failures or security issues.

tasks.title: Implement Text Analysis API
tasks.description: Create the core API for analyzing text content against policy rules with structured violation reporting.
tasks.details:
Implement the Text Analysis API with the following components:

1. API Endpoint:
   - Create POST /api/analyze endpoint
   - Implement request validation using Pydantic
   - Support the request schema specified in the PRD
   - Add authentication and rate limiting middleware

2. GPT-4 Integration:
   - Use OpenAI Python SDK 1.0+
   - Create prompt engineering for policy violation detection
   - Example prompt structure:
   ```
   You are a compliance violation detector. Analyze the following text against these policy rules:
   
   RULES:
   {serialized_rules}
   
   TEXT TO ANALYZE:
   {input_text}
   
   For each violation, provide:
   1. The rule_id that was violated
   2. A description of the violation
   3. A specific recommendation to fix the issue
   4. A confidence score (0.0-1.0)
   5. The severity level from the rule
   
   Format your response as a JSON array of violation objects.
   ```

3. Rule Matching:
   - Retrieve active rules for the team
   - Filter rules based on context (source, channel, etc.)
   - Serialize rules for inclusion in prompt

4. Response Processing:
   - Parse GPT-4 response into structured violation objects
   - Filter results based on confidence threshold (e.g., >0.7)
   - Format response according to schema in PRD
   - Store violations in violation_logs table

5. Performance Optimization:
   - Implement request caching using content hash
   - Add async processing for non-blocking operation
   - Optimize prompt size to reduce token usage

6. Usage Tracking:
   - Log analysis requests with token counts
   - Track usage against subscription quotas
   - Implement overage handling

Ensure the API meets the 2-second response time requirement specified in the PRD.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test API with various text inputs and rule sets
2. Measure response times under different loads
3. Verify violation detection accuracy with known test cases
4. Test caching mechanism with repeated requests
5. Validate usage tracking against subscription limits
6. Test error handling for various failure scenarios
7. Verify database logging of violations
8. Test concurrent requests for stability

subtasks.title: API Endpoint and Request Validation
subtasks.description: Design and implement the POST /api/analyze endpoint. Use Pydantic to enforce request schema validation, ensuring all required fields and data types are checked before processing. Return standardized error messages for validation failures.
subtasks.details: Define clear validation rules, validate inputs early, and automate validation using Pydantic. Ensure error responses are consistent and informative for developers.[1][2]

subtasks.title: Authentication and Rate Limiting
subtasks.description: Integrate authentication middleware to secure the endpoint, using JWT or API keys as appropriate. Implement rate limiting to prevent abuse and ensure fair usage.
subtasks.details: Standardize JWT validation using established libraries, avoid mixing authentication methods, and ensure all endpoints are protected. Apply rate limiting per user or API key.[3]

subtasks.title: GPT-4 Prompt Engineering
subtasks.description: Develop and refine prompt templates for GPT-4 to maximize accuracy in policy violation detection. Ensure prompts are structured, clear, and optimized for compliance analysis.
subtasks.details: Iterate on prompt design to improve detection reliability. Include context, rules, and expected output format in the prompt.

subtasks.title: GPT-4 API Integration
subtasks.description: Integrate the OpenAI Python SDK to send analysis requests to GPT-4. Handle API responses, errors, and retries as needed.
subtasks.details: Ensure secure handling of API keys, manage request/response lifecycle, and implement error handling for API failures.

subtasks.title: Rule Matching and Serialization
subtasks.description: Implement logic to match input text against policy rules and serialize rule definitions for use in prompt construction and response parsing.
subtasks.details: Support flexible rule formats and ensure serialization is compatible with both prompt engineering and downstream processing.

subtasks.title: Response Parsing and Filtering
subtasks.description: Parse GPT-4 responses to extract structured violation data. Filter and format results according to the API response schema.
subtasks.details: Implement robust parsing logic to handle variations in GPT-4 output. Validate parsed data before returning to clients.

subtasks.title: Performance Optimizations (Caching, Async)
subtasks.description: Optimize API performance by implementing caching for repeated requests and using asynchronous processing where appropriate.
subtasks.details: Cache analysis results for identical requests, use async I/O for external API calls, and monitor for bottlenecks under load.[1]

subtasks.title: Usage Tracking and Logging
subtasks.description: Implement comprehensive usage tracking and logging for all analysis requests, including authentication context and rate limit status.
subtasks.details: Log request metadata, track usage per user or API key, and ensure logs are structured for auditability and monitoring.

tasks.title: Implement File Upload Analysis
tasks.description: Support PDF file upload for compliance analysis, extracting text and processing through the standard analysis pipeline.
tasks.details:
Implement the File Upload Analysis feature with the following components:

1. API Endpoint:
   - Create POST /api/analyze/file endpoint
   - Support multipart/form-data for file uploads
   - Validate file types (PDF only initially)
   - Implement 10MB file size limit

2. PDF Text Extraction:
   - Use PyPDF2 3.0+ or pdfminer.six 20221105+ for text extraction
   - Handle various PDF formats and encodings
   - Implement error handling for corrupt or password-protected files
   - Extract text while preserving document structure where possible

3. Text Processing:
   - Chunk extracted text if it exceeds token limits
   - Process each chunk through standard analysis pipeline
   - Merge results from multiple chunks
   - Remove duplicates and consolidate similar violations

4. Response Formatting:
   - Return results in same format as text analysis API
   - Include document metadata in response
   - Provide page references for violations when possible

5. Performance Considerations:
   - Implement background processing for large files
   - Add progress tracking for long-running extractions
   - Cache extraction results to avoid reprocessing

6. Frontend Integration:
   - Create file upload component with drag-and-drop support
   - Show upload progress indicator
   - Display processing status updates
   - Present results in user-friendly format

Ensure proper error handling for various file-related issues and maintain consistent response format with the text analysis API.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test file upload with various PDF types and sizes
2. Verify text extraction accuracy from different PDF formats
3. Test handling of corrupt or invalid files
4. Measure processing time for various file sizes
5. Validate chunking and result merging for large documents
6. Test concurrent file uploads
7. Verify error handling for various failure scenarios
8. Test frontend integration and user experience

tasks.title: Implement Violations Feed Dashboard
tasks.description: Create a paginated view of all detected violations with filtering, resolution marking, and export functionality.
tasks.details:
Implement the Violations Feed Dashboard with the following components:

1. UI Implementation:
   - Create React component for /dashboard/violations route
   - Implement responsive table layout using Tailwind CSS
   - Add filtering controls for severity, source, date range
   - Implement pagination with 20 items per page
   - Add sorting functionality for columns

2. Data Fetching:
   - Create GET /api/violations endpoint with query parameters
   - Implement React Query for data fetching with caching
   - Add infinite scrolling or pagination controls
   - Support filtering via URL parameters

3. Violation Details:
   - Show key information: timestamp, source, severity, description
   - Add expandable rows for additional details
   - Include recommendation text
   - Show source context when available

4. Resolution Handling:
   - Add toggle or button to mark violations as resolved
   - Implement batch resolution for multiple items
   - Create PUT /api/violations/{id}/resolve endpoint
   - Add visual indicators for resolved vs. open violations

5. Export Functionality:
   - Implement CSV export of filtered violations
   - Create GET /api/violations/export endpoint
   - Add download button with loading indicator
   - Include all relevant fields in export

6. Real-time Updates:
   - Add WebSocket or polling for new violations
   - Implement toast notifications for new items
   - Update counts and filters automatically

Ensure the interface is responsive and follows the design principles outlined in the PRD.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test data loading with various filter combinations
2. Verify pagination works correctly
3. Test resolution marking for individual and batch items
4. Validate CSV export functionality
5. Test real-time updates for new violations
6. Verify filter persistence across page refreshes
7. Test responsive layout on different screen sizes
8. Validate sorting functionality for all columns

subtasks.title: Violations Table UI Implementation
subtasks.description: Design and implement the main violations table using a responsive layout. Integrate with a UI library (e.g., MUI, Ant Design, or Tailwind CSS) to ensure accessibility and scalability.
subtasks.details: Create a React component for the /dashboard/violations route. Ensure the table supports dynamic data rendering and is visually consistent with the dashboard's design system.

subtasks.title: Filtering and Sorting Controls
subtasks.description: Add filtering controls for severity, source, and date range, as well as sorting functionality for all relevant columns.
subtasks.details: Implement dropdowns, date pickers, and column header sort toggles. Ensure controls are intuitive and update the table data in real time.

subtasks.title: Pagination and Infinite Scroll
subtasks.description: Implement pagination with 20 items per page and support for infinite scroll as an alternative for large datasets.
subtasks.details: Add pagination controls below the table and/or infinite scroll logic. Ensure smooth user experience and efficient data loading.

subtasks.title: Data Fetching and Caching
subtasks.description: Integrate data fetching from the /api/violations endpoint using React Query or a similar library, with caching and query parameter support.
subtasks.details: Ensure data is fetched efficiently, supports all filter/sort/pagination parameters, and is cached for performance. Handle loading and error states gracefully.

subtasks.title: Violation Details and Expandable Rows
subtasks.description: Enable expandable rows in the table to show detailed violation information without navigating away.
subtasks.details: Implement row expansion UI and fetch or display additional details as needed. Ensure accessibility and responsiveness.

subtasks.title: Resolution Marking and Batch Operations
subtasks.description: Allow users to mark violations as resolved individually or in batches, with appropriate UI controls and feedback.
subtasks.details: Add checkboxes for selection, batch action buttons, and confirmation dialogs. Update backend and UI state accordingly.

subtasks.title: CSV Export and Download
subtasks.description: Implement functionality to export the current table view (with filters applied) as a CSV file.
subtasks.details: Add an export button, generate CSV data client-side or via API, and trigger download. Ensure correct formatting and encoding.

subtasks.title: Real-time Updates and Notifications
subtasks.description: Add real-time updates for new violations and notifications for relevant events using WebSockets or polling.
subtasks.details: Integrate a real-time data source, update the table as new violations arrive, and display notifications for critical events.

tasks.title: Implement Interactive Analysis Playground
tasks.description: Create an interface for ad-hoc compliance testing with text input, source selection, and PDF upload capabilities.
tasks.details:
Implement the Interactive Analysis Playground with the following components:

1. UI Implementation:
   - Create React component for /dashboard/analyze route
   - Implement tabbed interface for text vs. file analysis
   - Add text area with character counter (10,000 char limit)
   - Create source dropdown selection (slack, github, custom)
   - Add file upload zone with drag-and-drop support

2. Text Analysis:
   - Connect text input to /api/analyze endpoint
   - Implement debounced analysis for typing
   - Add "Analyze" button for manual submission
   - Show loading indicator during analysis

3. File Analysis:
   - Connect file upload to /api/analyze/file endpoint
   - Support PDF files up to 10MB
   - Show upload and processing progress
   - Handle various error states

4. Results Display:
   - Create violation card components
   - Group violations by severity
   - Show detailed information for each violation
   - Highlight relevant text portions when possible

5. Save Functionality:
   - Add button to save results to violations feed
   - Implement confirmation dialog
   - Create POST endpoint to save playground results
   - Show success/error notifications

6. History Feature:
   - Keep track of recent analyses
   - Allow quick loading of previous inputs
   - Implement local storage for persistence

Ensure the interface is intuitive and provides immediate feedback on analysis results.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test text analysis with various inputs and sources
2. Verify file upload and analysis works correctly
3. Test character limit enforcement
4. Validate results display for different violation types
5. Test save functionality to violations feed
6. Verify history feature retains recent analyses
7. Test error handling for various failure scenarios
8. Validate responsive layout on different screen sizes

tasks.title: Implement Usage & Billing Dashboard
tasks.description: Create a dashboard displaying current usage statistics, subscription details, and billing information with Stripe integration.
tasks.details:
Implement the Usage & Billing Dashboard with the following components:

1. UI Implementation:
   - Create React component for /dashboard/usage route
   - Design responsive layout with usage metrics and billing details
   - Implement progress bars for usage visualization
   - Add warning indicators for approaching limits (80%)

2. Usage Statistics:
   - Create GET /api/usage endpoint
   - Track API calls used vs. limit
   - Monitor token consumption metrics
   - Calculate percentage utilization
   - Show historical usage trends

3. Subscription Details:
   - Create GET /api/billing endpoint
   - Display current plan information
   - Show billing period and next payment date
   - List included features and limits
   - Add upgrade/downgrade options

4. Stripe Integration:
   - Implement Stripe Customer Portal integration
   - Add direct link to manage payment methods
   - Create webhook handler for Stripe events
   - Process subscription changes and updates

5. Overage Handling:
   - Display overage charges if applicable
   - Implement overage alerts and notifications
   - Add auto-upgrade options for frequent overages

6. Usage Breakdown:
   - Show usage by integration (Slack, GitHub, API)
   - Display usage by team member
   - Add date range selector for historical data
   - Create exportable usage reports

Ensure proper error handling for API failures and implement caching for performance.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test usage statistics calculation and display
2. Verify subscription details are accurate
3. Test Stripe Customer Portal integration
4. Validate warning indicators at threshold levels
5. Test webhook handling for Stripe events
6. Verify usage breakdown by different dimensions
7. Test responsive layout on different screen sizes
8. Validate export functionality for usage reports

tasks.title: Implement API Key Management
tasks.description: Create functionality to generate and manage API keys for external integrations with tracking and revocation capabilities.
tasks.details:
Implement the API Key Management feature with the following components:

1. UI Implementation:
   - Create React component for /dashboard/api-keys route
   - Design table view of existing API keys
   - Add creation form with name input
   - Implement revocation controls
   - Show usage statistics per key

2. API Key Generation:
   - Create POST /api/api-keys endpoint
   - Generate secure API keys with 32-byte entropy
   - Use crypto.randomBytes or similar for generation
   - Store key hash (not plaintext) in database
   - Return full key only once during creation

3. API Key Listing:
   - Create GET /api/api-keys endpoint
   - Return list of active keys with metadata
   - Include creation date, last used date
   - Show usage statistics

4. Key Revocation:
   - Create DELETE /api/api-keys/{id} endpoint
   - Implement soft deletion (revocation flag)
   - Add confirmation dialog in UI
   - Handle active requests gracefully

5. Usage Tracking:
   - Update authentication middleware to track key usage
   - Record last accessed timestamp
   - Count requests per key
   - Implement per-key rate limits

6. Security Measures:
   - Add key rotation recommendations
   - Implement automatic expiration for unused keys
   - Add IP restriction options (optional)
   - Log suspicious usage patterns

Ensure proper error handling and implement confirmation steps for security-sensitive operations.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test API key generation for proper entropy
2. Verify key listing shows correct metadata
3. Test revocation functionality
4. Validate usage tracking accuracy
5. Test rate limiting per key
6. Verify security measures function correctly
7. Test authentication using generated keys
8. Validate error handling for various scenarios

tasks.title: Implement Onboarding Flow and User Experience
tasks.description: Create a comprehensive onboarding experience for new users, including framework selection, integration setup, and initial policy configuration.
tasks.details:
Implement the Onboarding Flow with the following components:

1. Signup and Welcome:
   - Enhance Clerk signup with custom fields
   - Create welcome screen with product overview
   - Implement progress tracker for onboarding steps
   - Add ability to skip steps and return later

2. Compliance Framework Selection:
   - Create interface to select frameworks (GDPR, SOC 2, HIPAA)
   - Provide brief descriptions of each framework
   - Allow multiple selections
   - Pre-populate policy rules based on selections

3. Policy Document Upload:
   - Create guided interface for policy upload
   - Support template selection for common frameworks
   - Show parsing progress and results
   - Allow manual adjustments to parsed rules

4. Integration Setup:
   - Create step-by-step guides for Slack and GitHub integration
   - Implement OAuth flows with clear instructions
   - Show connection status and verification
   - Provide troubleshooting tips for common issues

5. Team Configuration:
   - Allow adding team members via email
   - Set up roles and permissions
   - Configure notification preferences
   - Set default monitoring settings

6. First Analysis:
   - Guide users to run their first analysis
   - Provide sample text with known violations
   - Show detailed explanation of results
   - Celebrate completion of onboarding

7. Persistent Onboarding State:
   - Track completion status of each step
   - Allow resuming onboarding process
   - Provide shortcuts to complete missing steps

Ensure the onboarding flow follows the design principles in the PRD and supports the "Time to First Value" KPI of <2 hours.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test end-to-end onboarding flow completion
2. Verify framework selection affects policy rules
3. Test policy document upload and parsing
4. Validate integration setup processes
5. Test team member invitation flow
6. Verify first analysis guidance
7. Test persistence of onboarding state
8. Validate responsive layout on different screen sizes

subtasks.title: Design Signup and Welcome UI
subtasks.description: Create a user-friendly signup interface with enhanced Clerk signup fields and a welcoming screen that introduces the product and its value.
subtasks.details: Include a brief product overview, clear calls to action, and a microsurvey or goal selection to personalize the onboarding journey.

subtasks.title: Implement Progress Tracker and Persistent State
subtasks.description: Develop a visual progress tracker for onboarding steps and ensure user progress is saved, allowing users to pause and resume onboarding.
subtasks.details: Use progress bars, checklists, and persistent storage to communicate status and reduce user anxiety during multi-step onboarding.

subtasks.title: Build Compliance Framework Selection Interface
subtasks.description: Create an interface for users to select one or more compliance frameworks (e.g., GDPR, SOC 2, HIPAA) with descriptions and multi-select capability.
subtasks.details: Pre-populate policy rules based on selected frameworks and provide contextual help for each option.

subtasks.title: Enable Policy Document Upload and Parsing
subtasks.description: Allow users to upload policy documents (PDF) and implement backend parsing to extract and analyze content.
subtasks.details: Support drag-and-drop upload, validate file types and size, and display parsing results or errors to the user.

subtasks.title: Develop Integration Setup Guides for Slack and GitHub
subtasks.description: Provide step-by-step guides and UI for connecting Slack and GitHub integrations, including OAuth flows and permission explanations.
subtasks.details: Include visual cues, error handling, and confirmation of successful integration setup.

subtasks.title: Implement Team Configuration and Invitations
subtasks.description: Create UI and backend logic for team management, including inviting members, assigning roles, and configuring permissions.
subtasks.details: Support email invitations, role selection, and real-time updates to team composition.

subtasks.title: Guide Users Through First Analysis
subtasks.description: Provide interactive guidance for users to run their first compliance analysis, highlighting key actions and expected outcomes.
subtasks.details: Use tooltips, checklists, and contextual help to ensure users understand the analysis process.

subtasks.title: Enable Manual Rule Adjustment
subtasks.description: Allow users to review and manually adjust compliance rules and policies after initial setup.
subtasks.details: Provide an intuitive editor with explanations, validation, and the ability to revert changes.

subtasks.title: Set Up Notification and Monitoring Preferences
subtasks.description: Implement configuration for notifications and monitoring, allowing users to choose alert channels and thresholds.
subtasks.details: Support Slack, email, and in-app notifications, with options for customizing frequency and severity levels.

subtasks.title: Ensure Responsive Design and Accessibility
subtasks.description: Review and enhance the onboarding flow UI for responsiveness and accessibility across devices and assistive technologies.
subtasks.details: Conduct accessibility audits, implement ARIA roles, and test on various screen sizes and input methods.

tasks.title: Implement Performance Optimization and Monitoring
tasks.description: Optimize application performance and implement monitoring to meet the <2 second response time requirement and ensure system reliability.
tasks.details:
Implement Performance Optimization and Monitoring with the following components:

1. Backend Optimization:
   - Implement database query optimization
   - Add database indexing for frequently queried fields
   - Implement connection pooling configuration
   - Use async processing for non-blocking operations
   - Add caching layer with Redis 7.0+

2. Frontend Optimization:
   - Implement code splitting with React.lazy
   - Add component-level memoization
   - Optimize bundle size with webpack analyzer
   - Implement progressive loading strategies
   - Add service worker for asset caching

3. API Performance:
   - Implement response compression
   - Add HTTP/2 support
   - Optimize serialization/deserialization
   - Implement batch operations for multiple items
   - Add pagination for large result sets

4. Monitoring Setup:
   - Implement application logging with structured format
   - Add request timing middleware
   - Set up health check endpoints
   - Implement error tracking with Sentry 7.0+
   - Create custom metrics for key operations

5. Alerting System:
   - Configure alerts for response time thresholds
   - Set up error rate monitoring
   - Implement usage spike detection
   - Add integration availability checks
   - Create on-call rotation for critical alerts

6. Performance Testing:
   - Implement load testing scripts
   - Create performance benchmarks
   - Set up continuous performance testing
   - Add synthetic monitoring for critical paths

Ensure the system meets the 99.5% uptime SLA and <2 second response time requirements specified in the PRD.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Measure response times under various load conditions
2. Test database query performance with large datasets
3. Verify caching effectiveness
4. Validate frontend loading performance
5. Test monitoring system captures performance issues
6. Verify alerting triggers for threshold violations
7. Measure system recovery after simulated failures
8. Validate health check endpoints accurately reflect system status

subtasks.title: Backend Performance Optimization
subtasks.description: Analyze and optimize backend systems for improved response times. Implement database query optimization, add indexing, configure connection pooling, introduce asynchronous processing, and integrate a Redis caching layer.
subtasks.details: Focus on reducing database latency, optimizing queries, and ensuring efficient resource utilization. Validate improvements with profiling tools and load testing.

subtasks.title: Frontend Performance Optimization
subtasks.description: Enhance frontend performance by implementing code splitting, component-level memoization, and bundle size optimization. Minimize HTTP requests, enable compression, and optimize images and static assets.
subtasks.details: Use React.lazy for code splitting, webpack for bundle optimization, and image compression tools. Ensure minimal use of third-party libraries and unnecessary plugins.

subtasks.title: API Performance Improvements
subtasks.description: Optimize API endpoints for speed and scalability. Reduce payload sizes, enable efficient data serialization, and implement caching strategies for frequently accessed endpoints.
subtasks.details: Profile API response times, refactor slow endpoints, and introduce rate limiting where necessary. Ensure APIs are stateless and scalable.

subtasks.title: Monitoring Setup (Logging, Metrics)
subtasks.description: Establish comprehensive monitoring by integrating logging and metrics collection across backend and frontend components.
subtasks.details: Deploy tools for centralized log aggregation and metrics visualization (e.g., ELK stack, Prometheus, Grafana). Ensure logs capture performance-relevant events and metrics track latency, throughput, and resource usage.

subtasks.title: Alerting System Configuration
subtasks.description: Configure alerting mechanisms to notify stakeholders of performance degradations or system anomalies.
subtasks.details: Set up alert thresholds for key metrics (e.g., response time, error rates) and integrate with notification channels such as Slack or email.

subtasks.title: Performance Testing and Benchmarking
subtasks.description: Conduct systematic performance testing and benchmarking to validate optimizations and identify bottlenecks.
subtasks.details: Use tools like JMeter or k6 for backend load testing, Lighthouse for frontend, and custom scripts for API benchmarking. Document baseline and post-optimization metrics.

subtasks.title: Health Checks and Uptime Monitoring
subtasks.description: Implement automated health checks and continuous uptime monitoring for all critical services.
subtasks.details: Deploy health endpoints, configure external uptime monitoring services, and ensure failures are logged and trigger alerts.

subtasks.title: Error Tracking and Recovery
subtasks.description: Integrate error tracking systems and establish automated recovery procedures for common failure scenarios.
subtasks.details: Use tools like Sentry for error tracking, implement retry logic, and document recovery playbooks for rapid incident response.
